parts.title: The Failing Greenhouse
parts.text: Orla stared at the crumbling greenhouse behind the school. The glass was cracked, the frame was rusting, and the plants had begun to **deteriorate** from weeks of neglect. She knew the place needed a complete **overhaul** if it was ever going to grow anything again. Renzo agreed that only a total **upheaval** of the old design would save it.

parts.title: The Plan Takes Shape
parts.text: Kenji arrived with a notebook full of sketches. He wanted to **reconfigure** the layout so that sunlight could reach every corner. Orla suggested they **dismantle** the broken shelves and rebuild them from recycled wood. Renzo's idea was even bolder — he wanted to **revolutionize** the watering system by using a simple solar-powered pump. Each small **modification** they discussed brought the group closer to a real plan.

parts.title: Transformation Begins
parts.text: Over the next two weeks, the trio watched an amazing **metamorphosis** unfold. They managed to **transmute** piles of scrap metal into sturdy plant racks. Kenji used colored glass to **transfigure** the dull walls into a rainbow of light. The slow **progression** from ruin to beauty amazed everyone who walked by. Finally, they decided to **consolidate** all their leftover supplies into one shared storage bin.

parts.title: A Living Laboratory
parts.text: On reopening day, the whole school gathered around. Seedlings stretched toward the sunlight, and butterflies drifted through the open windows. The three friends realized the greenhouse was more than a building — it was proof that patience, teamwork, and a good plan could turn something broken into something extraordinary.

definitions.meaning1: A complete change in form or structure. Example: The metamorphosis of a caterpillar into a butterfly is one of nature's greatest wonders.
definitions.studentChallenge: The tadpole went through a remarkable ___ before becoming a frog.

definitions.meaning1: A sudden, violent change or disruption. Example: The earthquake caused an upheaval that cracked roads and toppled buildings.
definitions.meaning2: A big change in the way things are done. Example: Moving to a new country was a major upheaval for the whole family.
definitions.studentChallenge: The sudden ___ in the schedule left everyone confused about where to go.

definitions.meaning1: To change the appearance or form of something, usually in a beautiful or dramatic way. Example: The artist managed to transfigure an old junkyard into a stunning sculpture garden.
definitions.studentChallenge: The stage crew used paint and fabric to ___ the gym into a magical forest.

definitions.meaning1: To change something into a different form or substance. Example: Ancient alchemists dreamed of being able to transmute lead into gold.
definitions.studentChallenge: The chef seemed to ___ simple ingredients into an incredible meal.

definitions.meaning1: To completely change the way something is done or thought about. Example: The invention of the internet helped revolutionize how people communicate around the world.
definitions.studentChallenge: New technology could ___ the way doctors treat patients in the future.

definitions.meaning1: A small change or adjustment made to improve something. Example: The teacher made a modification to the test so students had more time to finish.
definitions.studentChallenge: With one simple ___ to the recipe, the cookies turned out perfectly.

definitions.meaning1: To become worse over time. Example: If you don't water a garden, the plants will slowly deteriorate and die.
definitions.studentChallenge: The old bridge began to ___ after years without repairs.

definitions.meaning1: To rearrange or set up something in a new way. Example: We had to reconfigure the classroom desks to make room for the science fair projects.
definitions.studentChallenge: The IT teacher helped us ___ the computers so they could connect to the new printer.

definitions.meaning1: The process of developing or moving forward step by step. Example: The progression from beginner to expert takes years of practice.
definitions.studentChallenge: Her ___ in math this year has been remarkable — she went from struggling to excelling.

definitions.meaning1: A thorough examination and repair of something. Example: The mechanic gave the old car a complete overhaul so it would run like new.
definitions.meaning2: To take apart and fix completely. Example: The city plans to overhaul the entire bus system next year.
definitions.studentChallenge: The team decided to give the clubhouse a complete ___ before the new season.

definitions.meaning1: To take something apart piece by piece. Example: The workers had to carefully dismantle the old playground before building the new one.
definitions.studentChallenge: Before moving, we had to ___ the bookshelf and pack each piece separately.

definitions.meaning1: To combine several things into one single, stronger whole. Example: The teacher asked us to consolidate our notes into one neat study guide.
definitions.meaning2: To strengthen or make more secure. Example: After the win, the team worked hard to consolidate their lead in the standings.
definitions.studentChallenge: We decided to ___ all our supplies into one big box to save space.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The caterpillar underwent a stunning _______ as it slowly changed into a colorful butterfly.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The neighborhood went through a _______ when the empty lots were turned into beautiful community gardens.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The sudden _______ in government leadership caused confusion across the entire country.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Moving to a different state was a major _______ for the family, changing everything about their daily routine.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The costume designer managed to _______ the plain dress into a glittering royal gown for the play.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: A fresh coat of snow seemed to _______ the entire park into a winter wonderland overnight.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Ancient alchemists spent years trying to _______ ordinary metals into pure gold.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The writer was able to _______ her sadness into a powerful poem that moved the whole audience.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Electric cars could _______ the way people travel and reduce pollution around the world.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The new app promises to _______ how students study by making practice more interactive and fun.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The engineer made a small _______ to the bridge design to make it safer during storms.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: With just one _______ to the recipe, the chef turned a good dish into a great one.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: If the roof is not repaired soon, the entire building will continue to _______.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Without regular practice, a musician's skills can _______ over time.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The teacher decided to _______ the seating chart so students could work with new partners.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The IT team needed to _______ the network after adding twenty new computers to the lab.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The _______ from crawling to walking is an exciting milestone for every toddler.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Scientists tracked the _______ of the disease to understand how it spread from town to town.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The mechanic gave the old truck a complete _______ so it could pass the safety inspection.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The school board voted to _______ the outdated science curriculum from top to bottom.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The crew began to _______ the old stage set, carefully removing each piece of scenery.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Before recycling the computer, the technician had to _______ it and sort the parts.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The two small libraries decided to _______ their book collections into one larger shared library.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: After winning three games in a row, the team worked to _______ their position at the top of the league.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage:
When the old science museum began to _(1)_, the city council knew something had to change. Cracked walls and flickering lights were signs that a full _(2)_ was overdue. The first step was to _(3)_ the outdated exhibits piece by piece and haul them away.

The lead architect wanted to _(4)_ the floor plan so every hall connected to a central garden. Her team planned to _(5)_ recycled steel into gleaming new display frames — proof that old materials could have new life. A single _(6)_ to the lighting system filled every room with natural sunlight.

As construction continued, the _(7)_ from dusty ruin to shining museum amazed the whole neighborhood. Artists were invited to _(8)_ the bare concrete walls into colorful murals showing the history of science. The new interactive exhibits promised to _(9)_ how children learn about biology, chemistry, and physics.

On opening day, visitors gasped at the complete _(10)_ of the building. What had once been dull and forgotten now glowed with energy. The mayor said the project proved that even a great _(11)_ can lead to something wonderful. The city voted to _(12)_ all future museum funding into one dedicated budget to keep the building strong for generations.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
